spec_match.rationale: Generates a literal `<script type="permissionconstraints">` block with structured `video` / `audio` constraints, mirroring the spec's child-element constraint model. The equivalent `getUserMedia` call is rendered alongside.

interactive.rationale: Twelve form controls (facingMode / resolution / frameRate / echoCancellation / noiseSuppression / sampleRate) drive the generated HTML and JS in real time.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a configurable form that emits both forms — that is what the page renders.

edge_cases.rationale: Standard fields are covered, but the spec's `min`/`max`/`ideal`/`exact` advanced constraint syntax is reduced to a single value, and there is no `advanced` array support — that drops a significant portion of the constraints model.

references.rationale: see-also has no chromestatus.com/feature/4926233538330624 link. The slug-recovery script relies on it being on every page.

design_system.rationale: Variables-only colours, no glyph icons, no raw hex.

openQuestions.title: Add the chromestatus reference link
openQuestions.detail: see-also lists only siblings — append https://chromestatus.com/feature/4926233538330624 so the self-heal script can find the ID.

openQuestions.title: Expose min / max / ideal / exact
openQuestions.detail: Real MediaTrackConstraints support `{ width: { min: 640, ideal: 1280, max: 1920 } }`. Add a `qualifier` dropdown next to each numeric field so the builder can emit the full range — that is where the spec gets interesting.

openQuestions.title: Add an `advanced` constraints branch
openQuestions.detail: Some apps fall back through an advanced[] array. Add a `+ advanced fallback` button that lets the user stack a secondary constraint set, then emits the full structure into both the HTML and JS variants.

openQuestions.title: Try-it button to actually request the stream
openQuestions.detail: Currently the builder is read-only. Add a `request this stream` button that copies the constraints into a live `<usermedia>` element and reports what the browser negotiates (via `track.getSettings()`).

openQuestions.title: Validate constraint JSON
openQuestions.detail: If a developer hand-edits the generated JSON (or it's invalid), the element silently does nothing. Add a JSON validation badge next to the output so authors see when the document is malformed.

summary: Nice form-driven authoring tool, but the model is too flat — flatten-to-value loses the min/max/ideal/exact richness that constraints actually offer. Also missing the chromestatus self-heal link.
